New roots help the plant absorb water and necessary nutrients from the environment.
This is very important for plant growth, especially during the flowering stage.
Roots help keep the tree standing, creating stability and reducing the risk of falling,
especially in strong wind environments or when the tree is tall.
The growth of new roots often stimulates the growth of other parts of the plant, including leaves and flowers.
The plant will have enough energy to grow and flower.
Although roots are not directly involved in photosynthesis,
they provide water and nutrients to leaves where photosynthesis takes place.
newly developed roots often signal that the plant is in healthy condition,
creating favorable conditions for flowering and blooming.
Roots also participate in the plant's respiration process,
